to be added to the application model.

members.name: WithRoleAssignments

summary.text: Assigns the specified roles to the given resource, granting it the necessary permissions on the target Azure Service Bus namespace. This replaces the default role assignments for the resource.

remarks.text: This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use

builder.text: The resource to which the specified roles will be assigned.

roles.text: The built-in Service Bus roles to be assigned.

target.text: The target Azure Service Bus namespace.

members.name: .ctor
members.signature: public AzureServiceBusQueueResource.AzureServiceBusQueueResource(string name, string queueName, AzureServiceBusResource parent)

summary.text: Represents a Service Bus Queue. Initializes a new instance of the
class.
Gets the connection string expression for the Azure Service Bus Queue.
A value that indicates whether this queue has dead letter support when a message expires.

ISO 8601 default message timespan to live value. This is the duration after which the message expires, starting from when the message is sent to Service Bus. This is the default value used when TimeToLive is not set on a message itself.
ISO 8601 timeSpan structure that defines the duration of the duplicate detection history. The default value is 10 minutes.
Queue/Topic name to forward the Dead Letter message.
Queue/Topic name to forward the messages.
ISO 8601 timespan duration of a peek-lock; that is, the amount of time that the message is locked for other receivers. The maximum value for LockDuration is 5 minutes; the default value is 1 minute.
The maximum delivery count. A message is automatically dead-lettered after this number of deliveries.
Gets the parent Azure Service Bus resource.
The queue name.
A value indicating if this queue requires duplicate detection.
A value that indicates whether the queue supports the concept of sessions.
Represents an Azure Service Bus resource.

configureInfrastructure.text: Callback to configure the Azure Service Bus resource.

summary.text: Represents an Azure Service Bus resource.

configureInfrastructure.text: Callback to configure the Azure Service Bus resource.

summary.text: Gets the connection string template for the manifest for the Azure Service Bus endpoint.
Gets the host name for the Service Bus namespace.
Gets the "id" output reference for the resource.
Gets a value indicating whether the Azure Service Bus resource is running in the local emulator.
Gets the "name" output reference for the resource.
Gets the port for the Service Bus namespace.

remarks.text: In container mode, resolves to the container's primary endpoint port. In Azure mode, return null.

members.name: .ctor
members.signature: public AzureServiceBusSubscriptionResource.AzureServiceBusSubscriptionResource(string name, string subscriptionName, AzureServiceBusTopicResource parent)

summary.text: Represents a Service Bus Subscription. Initializes a new instance of the
class.
Gets the connection string expression for the Azure Service Bus Subscription.
A value that indicates whether this queue has dead letter support when a message expires.

ISO 8601 default message timespan to live value. This is the duration after which the message expires, starting from when the message is sent to Service Bus. This is the default value used when TimeToLive is not set on a message itself.
Queue/Topic name to forward the Dead Letter message.
Queue/Topic name to forward the messages.
ISO 8601 timespan duration of a peek-lock; that is, the amount of time that the message is locked for other receivers. The maximum value for LockDuration is 5 minutes; the default value is 1 minute.
The maximum delivery count. A message is automatically deadlettered after this number of deliveries. default value is 10.
Gets the parent Azure Service Bus Topic resource.
A value that indicates whether the queue supports the concept of sessions.
The rules for this subscription.
The subscription name.

ISO 8601 default message timespan to live value. This is the duration after which the message expires, starting from when the message is sent to Service Bus. This is the default value used when TimeToLive is not set on a message itself.
ISO 8601 timeSpan structure that defines the duration of the duplicate detection history. The default value is 10 minutes.
Gets the parent Azure Service Bus resource.
A value indicating if this topic requires duplicate detection.
The topic name.
